fixes.description: Removed Photoperiod_LD and Light from exogenous_supply in all 30 LD-condition tests that ALSO have a gene_modifier != 1.0.
fixes.reason: The algebraic validator contains logic (validation_common.py lines 281-286) that unconditionally overrides comparison_baseline to 'mutant' whenever BOTH gene_modifiers (with any value != 1.0) AND exogenous_supply are present. The original LD encoding convention (exogenous_supply={Photoperiod_LD:1.0, Light:1.0}) therefore forced all LD gene-KO tests into a rescue-style comparison: perturbed-under-LD vs the SAME mutant with LD supply removed. This produced nonsensical ratios (e.g. T003 PRR37 LOF: baseline mutant-without-LD flowered at 0.0; perturbed mutant-with-LD flowered at 5e-4; ratio=2137 -> 'increased'). The biologically intended comparison is PRR37-KO-in-LD vs WT-in-LD. Because source nodes already default to 1.0, the WT baseline is already LD-permissive; the exogenous boost was therefore double-counting LD AND triggering the rescue-comparison bug. Removing Photoperiod_LD/Light from exogenous_supply in LD tests restores the intended WT comparison without changing the WT biology.
fixes.biological_justification: Under the v1.0 source-node convention, source value = gene_modifier (default 1.0) + exogenous_supply (default 0.0). A source at 1.0 represents the standard permissive condition; the LD extraction convention in this dataset specifies LD = exogenous_supply 1.0 on Photoperiod_LD/Light, but this was not required to make LD permissive - sources are already permissive at 1.0. The SD encoding correctly uses gene_modifier=0.0 on Photoperiod_LD to ACTIVELY DEPRESS photoperiod input. LD and 'normal' conditions therefore collapse to the same WT baseline in the algebraic framework, which is biologically appropriate because sorghum is a facultative SD plant that already flowers under WT conditions; SD shortens time-to-flower by releasing LD-specific repression.
fixes.doi: n/a (encoding fix - no DOI required per §1.1 Rule 1)
fixes.predicted_impact: Estimated +20% best-method accuracy (most PRR37/PhyB/Ghd7/PhyC LOF LD tests would flip from wrong-direction to correct).

results.reason: Best-method accuracy (RWR) improved from 64.9% to 85.7% (+20.8%). Algebraic +24.7%, ODE +14.3%. Single encoding fix; no structural changes. Well above the +0.5% threshold.
